meaning: n.<俚>[棒球]用力或猛烈的投掷vt.用力投掷,愤慨地说出,丢下,推翻vi.猛投,猛掷
examples: It took only one heave to hurl him into the river.
In this context, generosity has come to mean that you hurl money around like a drunken sailor.
They had but one goal in mind: hurl a one pound payload exactly one mile.
The media will be denied the endless opportunities to hurl embarrassing questions about plebs.
Hurl a pair of chopsticks in any direction and chances are they will land in something edible.
